team_context.cap_situation: healthy — ~$43M in space after initial free agency moves, with extensions for JSN and Witherspoon still pending. Entered offseason with ~$75M pre-signings. 3-4 defensive scheme.
team_context.trade_posture: Defending Super Bowl champions looking to run it back. Lost Kenneth Walker III, Boye Mafe, Coby Bryant, and Riq Woolen in free agency. Re-signed Shaheed, Jobe, Drake Thomas. Need RB, EDGE depth, and possibly CB depth. Only 4 draft picks in 2026. Sellers of older/expensive veterans at positions of depth, buyers of youth and picks to replenish.

players.name: Sam Darnold
players.performance_evidence: Led Seahawks to 14-3 record and Super Bowl LX title. Pro Bowl selection in 2025. Completed 67.5%+ of passes for 4000+ yards. Career turnaround from Jets/Panthers bust to franchise QB. Not elite but efficient and well-suited to Kubiak's West Coast scheme.

trade_value.reasoning: Super Bowl-winning QB on a team-friendly deal ($33.5M AAV is 18th among QBs) with 2 years of control. QB premium makes him extremely valuable. Dead cap of $25.6M makes trade nearly impossible anyway.

players.availability_reasoning: Just won the Super Bowl. GM Schneider said he wants Darnold 'for a long time.' Dead cap of $25.6M makes a trade financially ruinous. Extension discussions expected after 2026.

players.name: Drew Lock
players.performance_evidence: Career backup who has been with Seattle since 2022. Did not play meaningful snaps in 2025. Provides experienced insurance behind Darnold.

trade_value.reasoning: Veteran backup QB on an expiring deal. Minimal trade value — teams can find equivalent QBs in free agency. Worth a conditional 7th at best.

players.availability_reasoning: Backup QB with minimal dead cap. Seahawks also have Jalen Milroe as a developmental QB. Lock could be moved if a team needs a veteran backup, but there's little demand.

players.name: Zach Charbonnet
players.performance_evidence: Led Seahawks with 12 rushing TDs and 730 rushing yards in 2025 as part of a 50/50 timeshare with Walker. Effective power runner and capable receiver. However, tore ACL in divisional round (Jan 2026) and will miss significant time in 2026.

trade_value.reasoning: Coming off ACL tear, entering final year of rookie deal, and RB position is devalued. The injury tanks his value from what would have been a mid-4th equivalent to nearly nothing. Seahawks need him for when he returns.

players.availability_reasoning: Seahawks lost Kenneth Walker in free agency and desperately need Charbonnet when healthy. Won't trade him unless blown away. ACL recovery timeline means he's essentially untradeable right now anyway — no team wants to trade for an injured RB on an expiring deal.
players.injury_notes: Torn ACL in January 2026 divisional round. Surgery in February 2026. Recovery timeline 8-12 months; may not be ready for Week 1. Coach Macdonald said he's 'more optimistic than initially' about timeline.

players.name: Kenny McIntosh
players.performance_evidence: Depth RB who saw limited action behind Walker and Charbonnet. With Walker gone and Charbonnet rehabbing ACL, McIntosh could see elevated role in 2026.

trade_value.reasoning: Depth RB on a minimum-level deal. Minimal trade value. Would only be included as a throw-in piece.

players.availability_reasoning: Low-cost depth piece. Seahawks need bodies at RB with Charbonnet hurt and Walker gone, so actually less likely to trade him than you'd expect for a player of this caliber.

players.name: Jaxon Smith-Njigba
players.performance_evidence: 2025 NFL Offensive Player of the Year. Led NFL with 1,793 receiving yards on 119 receptions and 10 TDs. Set Seahawks franchise single-season receiving record. 36.6% of team receptions, 44.1% of team receiving yards — among highest shares ever. Only 23 years old. Extension-eligible with 5th-year option available.

trade_value.reasoning: Young elite WR on a rookie deal ($4.6M cap hit) with 2 years of control plus 5th-year option. This is the single most valuable non-QB trade asset in the NFL. His combination of age (23), production (OPOY), and cost control is unmatched. In practice, you'd need multiple 1st-round picks to even start the conversation.

players.availability_reasoning: Franchise cornerstone. OPOY at 23 on a rookie deal. Seahawks plan to extend him this offseason to a deal that could exceed $40M/year. Zero chance of trade. Schneider would sooner trade anyone else on the roster.

players.name: Cooper Kupp
players.performance_evidence: 47 receptions for 593 yards and 2 TDs in 16 games in 2025. Ranked 57th among WRs by PFN's impact metric. Only 70 targets on the season (77th in NFL). Still an elite blocker and mentor to JSN, but his days as a full-time producing WR are over. Super Bowl LVI MVP feels like a different era.

trade_value.reasoning: At 33 with declining production and a $17.5M cap hit, Kupp's trade value is minimal. His intangible value (blocking, mentorship, locker room) exceeds his on-field receiving production. A team might give a late-round pick if Seattle ate some salary.

players.availability_reasoning: Spotrac flagged Kupp as a potential cut candidate before his $9M salary guarantee locked in. At $17.5M cap hit for WR57-level production, he's overpaid. However, his playoff contributions and mentorship of JSN earned him another year. Restructure is more likely than trade.
players.injury_notes: History of ACL tear (2018), high ankle sprain requiring surgery (2022). Has been relatively healthy last 2 seasons but entering age-33 season.
players.trade_buzz: Spotrac noted Seattle could free up $9.5M by moving on before his salary guarantee kicked in. That deadline passed (Feb 13), so he's effectively locked in for 2026.

players.name: Rashid Shaheed
players.performance_evidence: 59 catches, 687 yards, 2 TDs total in 2025 (split between Saints and Seahawks). In 12 games with Seattle: 18 catches, 266 yards. Primary value is as a game-changing returner — scored kick and punt return TDs in regular season, plus a 95-yard KR TD in divisional round. 2x Pro Bowl returner. Deep threat speed but limited as a pure receiver.

trade_value.reasoning: Signed days ago to a 3-year, $51M deal with $34.7M guaranteed, reflecting his dual WR/returner value. Cap hit of $23M in Year 1 is steep. 3 years of control on a freshly-signed deal. Worth a mid-3rd based on production, but the guaranteed money makes him hard to move.

players.availability_reasoning: Just re-signed to a significant 3-year deal. Seattle isn't looking to trade him. However, if his receiving production doesn't improve, the $23M Year 1 cap hit could look problematic down the road. Dead cap of $20M makes 2026 trade essentially impossible.

players.name: AJ Barner
players.performance_evidence: Emerged as a reliable target in 2025 as a second-year player. Caught a key 26-yard TD in the Week 16 comeback vs. Rams. Still developing as a pass catcher and blocker. Projects as a solid starter entering Year 3.

trade_value.reasoning: Young TE on a cheap rookie deal with 3 years of control. Shows flashes but hasn't established himself as a difference-maker. Worth a 4th-round pick based on age, cost control, and upside.

players.availability_reasoning: Cheap young TE with upside on a Super Bowl roster. Seattle wouldn't sell low but would listen to strong offers. The team needs targets for Darnold and Barner is developing into one.

players.name: Elijah Arroyo
players.performance_evidence: Rookie in 2025 out of Miami. Spent time on injured reserve during the regular season but was designated for return. Limited stats as a rookie. High upside athletic TE still developing.

trade_value.reasoning: Day 2 pick in Year 2 of his rookie deal. Cheap and young but hasn't proven anything yet. Worth a late-round pick based purely on draft pedigree and cost control.

players.availability_reasoning: Young developmental piece the Seahawks invested a 3rd-round pick in. They'd want at least equal draft capital back to move him.
players.injury_notes: Missed time on injured reserve as a rookie in 2025. Returned for playoffs.

players.name: Charles Cross
players.performance_evidence: Started 62 games in 4 seasons. Anchored the OL for the Super Bowl run. The largest non-QB contract in Seahawks history ($104.4M extension). Compared to Walter Jones franchise legacy. Excellent pass protector for Darnold.

trade_value.reasoning: 25-year-old franchise LT on a team-friendly cap hit ($10.9M in 2026) of a 5-year deal. Premium position, premium age, premium contract. Dead cap of $43M makes trade literally impossible.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed a 4-year, $104.4M extension in January 2026. Dead cap of $43M. The highest-paid non-QB in franchise history. Zero chance of trade.
players.injury_notes: Hamstring injury caused him to miss final 3 regular season games in 2025. Returned for playoffs.

players.name: Grey Zabel
players.performance_evidence: 2025 1st-round pick from North Dakota State who started all 17 regular season games as a rookie at LG. Integral part of the Super Bowl-winning OL. Praised by coaches and teammates for his intelligence and physicality. Still developing but showed Day 1 readiness.

trade_value.reasoning: Young starting IOL on a cheap rookie deal with 4 years of control plus 5th-year option. 1st-round pedigree, Super Bowl starter as a rookie. Premium value for a guard given age and contract.

players.availability_reasoning: 2025 1st-round pick who started every game as a rookie on a championship team. On a 4-year rookie deal. Seahawks have no reason to trade a cornerstone OL piece.
players.injury_notes: Had an injury scare in Los Angeles (Week 11) but recovered and didn't miss games.

players.name: Jalen Sundell
players.performance_evidence: UDFA from Oklahoma State who earned the starting center job in 2025 after initially making the roster as a backup. Started throughout the Super Bowl run. Praised by Zabel and others for his development. Average starter — holds his own but not a standout.

trade_value.reasoning: Starting center on a minimum deal with 1 year left. The cheap contract adds value, but he's an UDFA-caliber player on an expiring deal. Worth a late-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: UDFA on a cheap deal. If Seattle drafts or signs a center, Sundell becomes expendable. However, the Seahawks value continuity on the OL and may keep him as a reliable placeholder.

players.name: Anthony Bradford
players.performance_evidence: 4th-round pick who has developed into a starting RG. Physical run blocker. Part of the OL unit that helped the Seahawks rush for 2,096 yards in 2025. Solid but not outstanding.

trade_value.reasoning: Young starting guard on a cheap final-year rookie deal. Below-average starter but serviceable. Worth a late 5th/early 6th.

players.availability_reasoning: Entering final year of rookie deal. If Seahawks draft or acquire a guard, Bradford could be moved. Not a core piece but a useful starter.

players.name: Abraham Lucas
players.performance_evidence: Started all 17 games in 2025 after missing significant time in 2023-2024 due to knee injury/surgery. Dominant as a rookie in 2022. The extension ($46M/3yr) reflects faith in his recovery. Part of the bookend tackles with Cross. Local product from Everett, WA.

trade_value.reasoning: Starting RT on a fair 3-year deal ($15.3M AAV, 13th among RTs) with team-friendly cap hit in 2026 ($8.5M). Healthy season in 2025 rebuilt his value. OT premium applies. Worth a mid-to-late 2nd.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed a 3-year extension in September 2025. Part of the franchise's OL investment alongside Cross. Seattle would need a strong offer (2nd-round pick) to even consider it.
players.injury_notes: Missed most of 2023-2024 with knee injury requiring surgery. Played full healthy season in 2025 for first time since rookie year. Long-term knee durability is a concern.

players.name: Leonard Williams
players.performance_evidence: Led all NFL interior linemen in sacks (11) and tackles for loss (16) in 2024. Had a 92-yard interception return TD. NFC Defensive Player of the Month in Dec/Jan. Pro Bowl selection. Still producing at an All-Pro level despite entering age-32 season. Anchor of the 'Dark Side' defense.

trade_value.reasoning: Elite production but massive $29.6M cap hit on an expiring deal limits value. Dead cap of $22.4M makes pre-June 1 trade essentially impossible. Extension candidate ($18M AAV projected). A contender would love him but can't absorb the cap hit.

players.availability_reasoning: Entering final year at $29.6M cap hit. Extension candidate per Spotrac. Seattle wants to keep him but the cap number is massive. Dead cap of $22.4M makes trade nearly impossible pre-June 1. Post-June 1 trade more feasible but Seahawks need him to defend their title.
players.trade_buzz: Spotrac identified Williams as an extension candidate. Seattle restructured his deal in Feb 2025 (converting $18.7M to signing bonus), adding void years. Extension at ~$18M APY projected.

players.name: Byron Murphy II
players.performance_evidence: 2nd-year DT out of Texas, named Big 12 Defensive Lineman of the Year in college. Started as a rookie alongside Leonard Williams. Showed flashes of disruptive ability. Still developing but expected to take a leap in Year 2. Compared to Ed Oliver at the draft.

trade_value.reasoning: Young 1st-round DT on a cheap rookie deal with 3 years of control plus 5th-year option. The upside and cost control make him extremely valuable even before he's fully arrived. Worth a late 1st based on draft pedigree and ceiling.

players.availability_reasoning: 2024 1st-round pick entering Year 2 on a cheap deal. Seahawks are building their defensive future around Murphy alongside Williams. Not available.
players.injury_notes: Hamstring injury in Week 3 of 2024 caused 3-game absence. Otherwise healthy.

players.name: Jarran Reed
players.performance_evidence: Veteran run-stuffing DT in his 10th NFL season, 2nd stint with Seattle. Had 7 sacks in limited action per Spotrac — impressive production as a rotational piece. Won Super Bowl LX. Placed on IR in November 2025 with wrist/thumb injuries requiring surgery.

trade_value.reasoning: 33-year-old rotational DL coming off injury, but on a reasonable contract. Spotrac noted he's 'a formidable depth player but may be expendable.' Worth a conditional late-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Spotrac explicitly identified Reed as expendable, noting there's '$4.5M to be freed up.' At 33, coming off wrist surgery, and with Byron Murphy ascending, Reed is a prime trade or release candidate. Only $3M dead cap makes a move easy.
players.injury_notes: Placed on IR in November 2025 with wrist and thumb injuries. Underwent surgery. Recovery status for 2026 unclear.
players.character_notes: Suspended 6 games in 2019 for personal conduct policy violation related to a 2017 domestic violence incident.
players.trade_buzz: Spotrac flagged Reed as a potential cap casualty.

players.name: DeMarcus Lawrence
players.performance_evidence: Pro Bowl selection in 2025 after a resurgent season. Key addition that 'transformed this defense' per Julian Love. Reunited with DC Aden Durde from Dallas. Elite run defender with pass rush ability. 12-year veteran, 5x Pro Bowler.

trade_value.reasoning: 34-year-old EDGE on a reasonable deal ($9.85M cap hit) but age severely limits value. Only $2M dead cap makes him easy to move. Pro Bowl production in 2025 boosts value, but he's ancient for an edge rusher. Worth a 3rd-rounder to a contender.

players.availability_reasoning: ESPN's Brady Henderson reported retirement is a real possibility for Lawrence, who turned 34 in April and just had his 6th child. If he decides to play, his contract is team-friendly and his dead cap ($2M) makes him easy to trade. Seahawks don't know his plans yet.
players.injury_notes: Missed most of 2024 season with foot injury in Dallas. Played full 2025 season without issues.
players.trade_buzz: Retirement is a real possibility per ESPN's Brady Henderson. 'People in DeMarcus Lawrence's camp were not sure' about his 2026 plans as of late February.

players.name: Drake Thomas
players.performance_evidence: Breakout 2025 season as a first-year starter. UDFA from NC State who worked his way up from backup. Made a pivotal play in the Seahawks' biggest regular season win. Re-signed on a 2-year, $8M deal (up to $9M) before free agency.

trade_value.reasoning: Young starting LB on a cheap deal with 2 years of control. Just re-signed, showing the team values him. Breakout player but not established enough for premium value. Worth a 4th-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Just re-signed on a team-friendly deal. Seattle views him as a core piece going forward. Would only trade for clear overpay.

players.name: Ernest Jones IV
players.performance_evidence: Key ILB for the Super Bowl defense. In Week 13 had 12 tackles, 2 INTs (one returned 85 yards for TD), and 2 PBUs. Best season was 2023 with Rams: 145 tackles, 4.5 sacks, 14 TFLs. Traded twice in 2024 (Rams to Titans to Seahawks) before finding a home in Seattle. 3-year, $28.5M deal signed March 2025.

trade_value.reasoning: 26-year-old starting ILB on a team-friendly deal ($9.5M AAV) with 2 years of control. Produced at a high level in Seattle's championship defense. LBs are undervalued in trade market but Jones' age and production warrant a late 2nd/early 3rd.

players.availability_reasoning: Core piece of the defense signed to a fair deal last year. Seattle re-signed him specifically to keep the defense intact. Dead cap of $7M makes a trade before 2027 costly. Not being shopped.

players.name: Uchenna Nwosu
players.performance_evidence: Had 7 sacks in 2025 playing limited snaps alongside Lawrence. Career-best was 9.5 sacks in 2022. But has battled significant injuries: torn pectoral (2023, season-ending after 6 games). Took a $7M pay cut in 2025 to stay. Due $20M cap hit in 2026 on a contract year.

trade_value.reasoning: Productive EDGE but $20M cap hit on an expiring deal is brutal. Spotrac says there's 'over $12M to free up with a trade or release.' A contender might give a mid-4th-round pick to rent him. Age (29) and injury history cap the ceiling.

players.availability_reasoning: Multiple analysts have flagged Nwosu as a cap casualty candidate. Spotrac specifically noted 'Nwosu is entering a contract year, and there's over $12M to free up with a trade or release.' The Seahawks lost Boye Mafe to FA but could look to younger/cheaper edge options. Release or restructure more likely than trade given dead cap.
players.injury_notes: Torn pectoral ended 2023 season after 6 games. Has battled various injuries since. Beat reporter noted 'I don't think Nwosu is getting back to his 2022 form. Too many injuries stacked up.'
players.trade_buzz: Gregg Rosenthal (NFL.com): 'I think they might end up cutting Uchenna Nwosu here to open up some more money.' Widely viewed as a cap casualty candidate.

players.name: Devon Witherspoon
players.performance_evidence: 3x Pro Bowler in first 3 seasons. First-team All-Pro in 2025. 90.1 PFF grade (best at his position). Lockdown corner who 'is almost never targeted.' 72 tackles, 7 PD despite missing 5 games in 2025. Elite versatility — outside CB, slot, blitzer. Sacked QB in Super Bowl LX. One of the best defensive players in the NFL.

trade_value.reasoning: 25-year-old All-Pro CB on a rookie deal ($10.1M cap hit) with 5th-year option available for 2027. CB premium applies. Extension-eligible — projected for $25-31M/year deal. In practice, it would take 2+ first-round picks to pry him away, which no team would offer for a CB.

players.availability_reasoning: Seahawks explicitly plan to extend both JSN and Witherspoon this offseason per ESPN's Brady Henderson. 3x Pro Bowler, All-Pro at 25 on a rookie deal. Face of the 'Dark Side' defense. Absolutely untouchable.
players.injury_notes: Missed 5 games in 2025. Hip injury in rookie year caused 2-game absence. Generally durable but has had minor absences.

players.name: Josh Jobe
players.performance_evidence: Started 15 of 16 games in 2025. Allowed 4th-lowest completion rate (47.7%) and 4th-fewest yards per target (5.4) among outside CBs with 50+ targets per Next Gen Stats. 71.1 passer rating allowed was 5th-best. 54 tackles, 12 PD, 1 INT in regular season. Rose from practice squad to full-time starter.

trade_value.reasoning: Solid CB2 on a 3-year, $24M deal ($8M AAV) with team-friendly Year 1 cap hit ($4.6M). Strong coverage metrics in 2025. Dead cap of $7M limits trade flexibility in Year 1. Worth a mid-3rd to a CB-needy team.

players.availability_reasoning: Just re-signed 3 days ago. Seattle specifically retained him to replace Riq Woolen as the CB2 opposite Witherspoon. Not being shopped but every player has a price.
players.injury_notes: Missed 1 game in 2025 with a concussion.

players.name: Nick Emmanwori
players.performance_evidence: Rookie in 2025, drafted 35th overall after Seahawks traded up. 6-3, 220 lbs, ran 4.38 at combine. Seahawks had a 1st-round grade on him. Used in big nickel/dime packages alongside Love and Bryant (now gone). Physically imposing with elite athleticism. Compared to Kam Chancellor and Kyle Hamilton.

trade_value.reasoning: 2nd-round pick entering Year 2 on a cheap deal with 4 years of control. Seahawks traded up aggressively to get him, indicating strong organizational conviction. Rare physical profile. High upside and cost control make him very valuable despite limited proof.

players.availability_reasoning: Seahawks traded up 17 spots to draft him, gave him a 1st-round grade, and are now deploying him as a nickel/safety hybrid in their championship defense. With Coby Bryant gone to Chicago, Emmanwori's role will only expand. Not available.

players.name: Ty Okada
players.performance_evidence: UDFA who was tendered as an exclusive rights free agent for 2026. Expected to step into a full-time starting role with Coby Bryant departing to Chicago. Budget starter who showed enough in limited opportunities to earn the Seahawks' confidence.

trade_value.reasoning: UDFA on a minimum deal entering a starting role for the first time. Unproven as a full-time starter. Worth a conditional late-round pick at best.

players.availability_reasoning: Cheap depth piece who could become a starter by default. If Seahawks draft or sign a safety, Okada becomes expendable. Low cost means low return.

players.name: Julian Love
players.performance_evidence: Pro Bowl selection in 2025. Quarterback of the secondary for the NFL's #1 scoring defense. Signed a 3-year extension with Seattle prior to the 2025 season. Vocal leader who praised teammates' contributions throughout the Super Bowl run. Consistent, high-IQ safety.

trade_value.reasoning: 28-year-old Pro Bowl FS on a fair deal ($10M AAV) with 2 years of control. Safety is a lower-value position in trades, but Love's leadership and production on a championship defense enhance his value. Worth a late 2nd/early 3rd.

players.availability_reasoning: Core piece of the defense signed to an extension. Seattle lost Coby Bryant in free agency, making Love even more important as the veteran anchor of the secondary. Not being shopped.
players.injury_notes: Was placed on injured reserve during 2025 season but returned for the stretch run and playoffs.
